claim_bullets.bullet_text: Collections in JavaScript expose some methods only on select types, while others are ubiquitous, illustrating a heterogeneous design (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.246-260) – might be unnecessary duplication.
Embedding mapping, filtering, and other operations inside each collection leads to a fat‑object style that can be avoided by delegating to generic iterables (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.246-260).
LazyCollection is a mixin that, when applied to any ordered iterable, provides lazy map, filter, reduce, find, and other operations (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.246-260).

The chapter argues object‑oriented collections should offer mapping, reducing, filtering, and finding methods, but delegating to a lazy iterator can reduce duplication (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.246-260).
Each collection traditionally implements its own .map, .reduce, .filter, and .find, causing duplicated code across types (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.246-260).

source_record_text: Summary of Lazy and Eager Collections chapter in JavaScriptAllonge – possible re‑use of a lazy iterator.
